data.title: 3 Best Trending Stocks, According to Analysts – 3/12/2026
data.description:
TipRanks' Trending Stocks tool allows users to filter activity over the past 30 days, seven days, or even the last 72 hours to see which stocks are drawing the most attention. Checking this page regularly can help you stay ahead of the latest market movers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here are the top three most‑rated stocks over the past 72 hours (listed in descending order). Click any ticker to explore all of TipRanks' unique datasets for that stock.

Oracle (ORCL) – This global technology company provides cloud computing services, enterprise software, and database solutions used by businesses. ORCL stock has been rated by 27 analysts in the past three days. This flood of ratings came after the company reported better-than-expected fiscal third-quarter earnings.

The analyst consensus on the stock is a Strong Buy. With an average price target of $256.90, the stock has an implied upside potential of 61.41%.

Fortinet (FTNT) – This cybersecurity company provides network security solutions such as firewalls, secure networking, and threat intelligence to protect organizations from cyber threats. The stock is also trending, having been rated 17 times in the past 72 hours. The ratings came after the company launched new products at the 2026 user conference and investor session.

The analyst consensus on the stock is a Hold, with an average price target of $87.61, suggesting an upside potential of 3.8%.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) – This biotechnology company develops innovative medicines for serious diseases, best known for its breakthrough treatments for cystic fibrosis. The stock has received 15 ratings over the past 72 hours after the company said its experimental drug for a rare kidney disease succeeded in a Phase 3 clinical trial.

It earned an analyst consensus of Strong Buy and an average price target of $556.87, indicating a 16.47% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Costco (COST) Faces a Class-Action Lawsuit Over This Divisive Political Issue
data.description:
Costco Wholesale (COST) faces a class-action lawsuit from its customers in Illinois who are demanding a tariff refund after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down U.S. President Donald Trump's import duties.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Costco is the first retailer to face a potential class-action lawsuit related to tariff refunds. A suit filed in Illinois federal court alleges that Costco owes its customers refunds in light of the Supreme Court's ruling against Trump's sweeping tariffs.

The lawsuit has been filed on behalf of a Costco member in the State of Illinois, Matthew Stockov, and seeks class-action status, meaning it has the potential to snowball into something bigger if certified by a judge. The class-action certification still needs to happen, but the lawsuit amounts to a big headache for Costco and possibly other retailers as well.

Costco's Lawsuit Against the Trump Administration
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Oracle's 'Restructuring Is Code for Layoffs,' Analyst Warns — Can ORCL Stock Keep Rising?
data.description:
Shares of Oracle (ORCL) jumped after the company beat fiscal Q3 expectations, with investors cheering the strong results and analysts reaffirming optimism. However, restructuring costs and potential layoffs are drawing attention. RBC Capital analyst Rishi Jaluria highlighted higher restructuring expenses, raising concerns about possible job cuts in the coming months. Looking ahead, analysts rate ORCL as a Strong Buy based on its Q3 performance with an average price target pointing to over 55% upside, though short-term upside may be limited by ongoing restructuring risks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Oracle reported Q3 earnings of $1.79 per share, beating the Street estimate of $1.70, with revenue up 22% year-over-year to $17.19 billion, also surpassing expectations.

Oracle Restructuring Sparks Layoff Fears

According to an SEC (Securities and Exchange Commission) filing on Wednesday, following its Q3 earnings report, Oracle has allocated an extra $500 million for restructuring costs. This brings total FY26 funds to $2.1 billion, far higher than in previous years, suggesting thousands of roles could be cut.

Additionally, the company said in its earnings statement that AI coding tools could let it do more with fewer developers, adding that the technology is "enabling us to build more software in less time with fewer people."

Last week, Bloomberg reported that Oracle is planning to cut thousands of jobs as it manages a cash crunch from its massive AI data center expansion. The planned reductions could affect multiple divisions across the company, reinforcing the link between the increased restructuring fund and the company's broader cost-management strategy.

RBC Flags Risks but Remains Bullish

Jaluria stated that a company doesn't expand its restructuring plan by $500 million for the coming quarter without planning job cuts. He added, "Oracle is signaling that restructuring, which is often code for layoffs, could be broader than earlier this fiscal year."

Nonetheless, Jaluria reiterated his Buy rating on ORCL stock, citing strong growth in results. He noted that sustained share gains will likely depend on disciplined spending, stable margins, and a clear path to free cash flow recovery.

Is ORCL Stock a Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on ORCL stock, based on 28 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average Oracle stock price target of $256.23 implies an upside of 57% from current levels.

Year-to-date, ORCL stock has declined by 16%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Tumble as Oil Approaches $100 on Iranian Leader's Vow to Keep Hormuz Closed
data.description:
It was another rough day for the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ), as both ETFs closed down by over 1.5% on Thursday as the U.S.-Iran war dragged on into its 13th day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • In his first publicly broadcast message as Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei said that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed as a "tool to pressure the enemy" and vowed to strike U.S. bases in the Middle East. Crude oil futures (CL) surged 10% to $95 following his comments.
  • The Trump administration announced trade investigations into 16 countries, including China, the European Union, Mexico, and India, under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974. The investigation centers on excess capacity and production and could lead to new tariffs and fines.
  • U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer noted that the U.S. would announce additional Section 301 trade investigations against 60 countries as soon as Thursday.
  • The U.S. trade deficit fell by 25.3% to $54.5 billion as exports jumped to a record high of $302.1 billion.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) announced a partnership with AMD (AMD) to develop its next-generation Xbox console.
  • Morgan Stanley (MS) closed in the red after the firm joined a growing number of financial firms to limit withdrawals from one of its private credit funds.
  • Palantir (PLTR) finished higher after announcing new collaborations with Nvidia (NVDA), GE Aerospace (GE), and Ondas (ONDS) at its AIPCon 9 event.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector for a second consecutive day amid rising oil prices and the risk of a prolonged closure to the Strait of Hormuz. Higher oil prices typically boost profits for oil producers, driving gains in stocks like Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and Chevron (CVX) as investors anticipate stronger earnings.

Several energy stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY)
  • Phillips 66 (PSX)
  • Chevron (CVX)
  • Marathon Petroleum (MPC)
  • Valero Energy (VLO)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, industrial stocks posted the worst performance on Thursday. With higher gas prices, transportation and travel companies face increased operating costs, weighing on profits and margins. The sector also struggled as investors assessed the impact of supply chain challenges and global trade uncertainty.

Notable industrial stocks trading lower include:

  • Southwest Airlines (LUV)
  • Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL)
  • GE Aerospace (GE)
  • United Rentals (URI)
  • Emerson Electric (EMR)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: ADBE Earnings: Adobe Stock Slips after Smashing Q1 Estimates
data.description:
Shares of Adobe (ADBE) fell in after-hours trading after the creative software company reported earnings for its first quarter of Fiscal Year 2026. Earnings per share came in at $6.06, which beat analysts' consensus estimate of $5.87 per share.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, sales increased by 12.1% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $6.4 billion. This beat analysts' expectations of $6.28 billion.

2026 Outlook

Looking forward, management has provided the following guidance for Q2 2026:

  • Revenue of between $6.43 billion and $6.48 billion, versus analysts' estimates of $6.43 billion
  • Adjusted EPS in the range of $5.80 to $5.85, compared to analysts' estimates of $5.68.

As we can see, the company's outlook is better than expected, but still wasn't enough to push the stock higher.

Is ADBE Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on ADBE stock based on six Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ADBE price target of $370.24 per share implies 37.2% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Elon Musk's xAI Loses Yet Another Founder
data.description:
Elon Musk's artificial intelligence startup xAI is seeing more departures from its founding team, according to a report from Business Insider. The latest exit involves Zihang Dai, who left the company earlier this week after serving as a member of the technical staff. His departure became noticeable after the xAI badge disappeared from his profile on X, the social media platform formerly known as Twitter.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, another co-founder, Guodong Zhang, has reportedly told colleagues that he also plans to leave the company soon, although his LinkedIn profile still lists him as working at xAI. Zhang previously worked at Google DeepMind (GOOGL) and played a major role at xAI by leading two important projects known as Grok Code and Grok Imagine. Earlier this year, he was even given additional responsibilities shortly before another co-founder, Tony Wu, left the company.

These departures add to a growing list of exits from the team that originally launched xAI with Musk in 2023. In recent months, other co-founders, including Toby Pohlen, Jimmy Ba, Tony Wu, and Greg Yang, have also left the company. If both Dai and Zhang depart, only two of the original eleven co-founders, Manuel Kroiss and Ross Nordeen, will remain at the startup. The leadership changes come as Musk continues to push the development of the company's Grok AI chatbot.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $399.25 per share implies that shares are trading near fair value.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Oil Nears $100 as Hormuz Remains in a Near Standstill
data.description:
Crude oil futures (CL) are up by over 10% on Thursday to $95 per barrel as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed on the 13th day of the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In his first public message, Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, said the shipping lane will remain closed as a "tool to pressure the enemy" and warned that U.S. bases in the Middle East will be attacked. Over the past two days, Iran has attacked seven tankers traveling through the strait and the Persian Gulf.

Oil Shrugs Off Massive IEA Reserve Release

The International Energy Agency (IEA) is set to release 400 million barrels of oil from the reserves of its member countries to combat higher prices. Historical oil releases have peaked at around 1.5 million barrels per day (bpd), much less than the roughly 15 million bpd disrupted by the closure of the strait.

Crude oil has now surged by over 40% since the start of the war, lifting gas prices in the process. Since March 1, the average price for a gallon of gas in the U.S. has increased by 70 cents, or 24%, to $3.59.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Extortionary Threats by the Government": Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Plunges on New Allegations
data.description:
Did chip stock Intel (INTC) agree to let the United States buy nearly 10% of its stock to avoid being called out on social media? That is what a new lawsuit alleges, and shareholders seem terribly unhappy about this notion, sending Intel shares careening down over 5% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reports noted that an Intel shareholder, Richard Paisner, filed suit against the company, alleging that Intel only sold 10% of the company to the government to avoid getting dragged on social media by the Trump administration. The lawsuit further declares the sale an "…unlawful contract that gives the US government $11 billion worth of Intel stock for no meaningful consideration in response to extortionary threats by the government."

I may not be a lawyer, but it would seem that $11 billion is "meaningful consideration" enough. At any rate, the suit continues, alleging that Intel's leadership was more concerned with "…protecting their personal reputations, being free from attacks by President Trump and his supporters on social media and elsewhere…" than with protecting Intel shareholders. The suit further alleged that the stock purchase deal was struck as a way to allow current CEO Lip-Bu Tan to keep his job. Intel, meanwhile, did not comment on the suit.

Not Running From RAN

Meanwhile, anyone who thought that Intel might have a plan in place to sell off its radio access network (RAN) business will be disappointed. Intel has no plan to sell off the networks business, reports note, as Intel processors are up to the task of handling the confluence of RAN and artificial intelligence (AI).

In fact, Intel looks for its Granite Rapids processor to do a particularly good job with the RAN market. The recent partnership efforts with Ericsson (ERIC) were again referenced, and Intel's move to be a useful part of the 6G market should have sat better with investors than it seems to have in today's trading.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on seven Buys, 22 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 102.45% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $47.97 per share implies 5.5% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Walmart Looking for New Fashion Guru to Keep Smartening up Sales
data.description:
Retail giant Walmart (WMT) is about to launch a fashion push to keep smartening up sales. The company is advertising for a new role at the company – Senior Manager, Marketing Planning and Strategy – Fashion Marketing.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

'Redefine' Walmart Fashion

In the job advertisement Walmart said the role would 'help redefine how customers see Walmart – transforming it into a true destination for style.' The Senior Manager, Walmart said, would lead the marketing vision for a 'fast-growing portfolio of private fashion brands, from exciting new launches to strategic renovations with the mission of elevating perception and attracting new customers.' It said that Walmart Fashion is a strategic growth priority.

In its recent Q4 earnings, Walmart said that fashion sales had been strong in the U.S. eCommerce sales grew double-digits with marketplace categories including fashion, cook & dine, and home decor up 40%.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Cyberattack Knocks Stryker Stock (SYK) — But Citi Sees Upside with '90-Day Catalyst Watch'
data.description:
Stryker Corporation (SYK) stock came under pressure after Iran-linked hackers seized 50 terabytes of sensitive data from the company in a cyberattack.  However, despite the short-term concerns, Citi's four-star-rated analyst Joanne Wuensch remains optimistic. She placed SYK stock on an "upside 90-day catalyst watch," suggesting potential for a rebound in the coming months. Following the cyberattack, SYK stock fell 3.59% on Wednesday and dropped another 0.97% in after-hours trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Stryker develops and sells devices, implants, and equipment used in surgery, orthopedics, and hospital care.

What Happened in the Stryker Cyberattack?

Stryker experienced a global network disruption affecting its Microsoft (MSFT) systems due to the attack. The incident disabled Windows-based laptops and mobile devices connected to its network. Interestingly, the cyberattack has drawn attention to a highly destructive type of hacking known as wiper malware. Unlike ransomware, which tries to demand payment, wiper malware is designed to erase data and shut down computer systems.

A hacking group known as Handala, which has reported ties to Tehran, has claimed responsibility for the attack. The group described the breach as retaliation and also called the incident "the beginning of a new chapter in cyber warfare."

In response, Stryker launched its cyber response plan, hired external experts, and put measures in place to keep operations running. The full impact on business and finances is still unknown, and recovery could take time. The company has not yet said if the incident will be material.

Why Is Citi Bullish on SYK Stock?

Citigroup's Wuensch has opened an "upside 90-day catalyst watch" on SYK while maintaining a Buy rating and a $420 price target. Her target implies an upside of over 20% from the current level. The 90-day catalyst watch likely reflects early data from the Mako RPS handheld robotic system, which began a limited market release in February 2026. Notably, Mako RPS is Stryker's new handheld robotic system aimed at expanding its orthopedic robotics portfolio with smaller, more accessible devices for total knee replacements.

Overall, Wuensch believes the U.S. medical technology sector remains "healthy," even with recent market volatility. She also noted that Stryker has delivered consistent double-digit growth in both revenue and earnings. Stryker reported net sales of $25.1 billion in 2025, up 11.2%, with organic net sales rising 10.3%. For 2026, the company expects organic net sales growth of 8.0%–9.5%.

Is SYK a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on SYK stock based on 17 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average Stryker stock price target of $429.50 implies 24.21% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak
